meta.title: Docs — GitFolder
meta.description: GitFolder documentation: install, add folders, sync settings, SSH keys, troubleshooting and FAQ. GitKanban docs live on GitHub.

sidebarNoteHtml: Looking for GitKanban? Its board format and docs live on GitHub.

items.title: Install GitFolder
items.html: Download GitFolder from the Mac App Store. It lives in your menu bar — no dock icon.

items.title: Make sure Git is installed
items.html: GitFolder uses your system Git. Open Terminal and run git --version to verify. If Git isn't installed, macOS will prompt you to install Xcode Command Line Tools.

items.title: Set up GitHub SSH
items.html: GitFolder pushes over SSH. Make sure ssh -T git@github.com works in Terminal. If you need to set up SSH keys, follow GitHub's SSH guide.

items.title: Mac App Store
items.html: Get GitFolder from the Mac App Store. It's a single purchase — no subscription.

items.title: Launch and open Settings
items.html: Click the folder icon in the menu bar, then click the gear icon to open Settings. This is where you configure Git identity and add folders.

items.title: Open Settings → Repositories
items.html: Click the folder icon in the menu bar, then the gear icon. Go to the Repositories tab and click "Add Repository…".

items.title: Choose a local folder
items.html: Use the macOS folder picker to select the folder you want to version. GitFolder will request access to this folder.

items.title: Paste a GitHub SSH URL
items.html: Enter the SSH URL of the repository you want to push to, like git@github.com:yourname/my-folder-backup.git.

items.title: Choose branch and interval
items.html: Pick a branch name (default: main) and a sync interval (5, 15, 30, or 60 minutes). Click "Add and Sync" — GitFolder will run the first snapshot.

blocks.html: Configure syncing behavior in Settings → General:

items: Pause all syncing — temporarily stop all folder snapshots
Default interval — the default sync interval for new folders (5, 15, 30, or 60 minutes)
Per-folder override — each folder can have its own interval, branch, and pause state

blocks.title: Sync All Now
blocks.html: Click "Sync All Now" in the General tab or in the menu bar to trigger an immediate snapshot for all active folders, regardless of their interval.
GitFolder uses SSH to push to GitHub. In most cases your existing SSH setup works out of the box.

items.html: Choose a specific key in Settings → SSH
Mac App Store builds may need explicit key selection

blocks.title: Mac App Store sandbox
blocks.html: Sandboxed apps can't access ~/.ssh directly. If you're using the Mac App Store version, go to Settings → SSH and choose your SSH private key file explicitly.

blocks.title: Folder shows "Error" status
blocks.html:
Check the error message in Settings → Repositories. Common causes:

  • SSH key not configured or not accessible
  • Repository URL is wrong or you don't have push access
  • Branch doesn't exist yet and can't be created
  • Git conflict — resolve it manually in your Git client, then GitFolder will resume

items.title: Verify Git is installed
items.html: Open Terminal and run git --version. If not found, install Xcode Command Line Tools with xcode-select --install.

items.title: Test SSH access
items.html: Run ssh -T git@github.com in Terminal. You should see your GitHub username in the response.

items.title: Check the repository URL
items.html: Make sure the URL in GitFolder matches the SSH URL shown on your GitHub repository page. It should start with git@github.com:.

items.q: Does GitFolder read my file contents?
items.html: No. GitFolder uses Git to track changes. Git creates commits from your files — GitFolder just triggers the process. Your file data goes directly to your GitHub repository over SSH.

items.q: Can I use it with GitLab, Bitbucket, or self-hosted Git?
items.html: GitFolder is designed for GitHub SSH URLs. Other Git hosts that support SSH may work if the URL format and authentication are compatible, but only GitHub is officially supported.

items.q: What happens if there's a merge conflict?
items.html: GitFolder pauses the folder and shows a "Needs attention" status. Resolve the conflict in your preferred Git client, then resume syncing in GitFolder.

items.q: Does it need a GitFolder account?
items.html: No. There is no GitFolder account, no cloud service, and no backend server. Everything runs locally on your Mac.
